Mar 19, 2026 # Gold Market Outlook: Diverging Analyst Views Amid Economic Data Analysts from several financial institutions have recently offered contrasting perspectives on the gold market, as indicated by a source from Kitco. JP Morgan has identified an argument suggesting the current rally in gold may not persist, though the firm reportedly disputes that view. ANZ has projected that gold could reach a price of $5,800 per ounce during the second quarter of this year. Other firms have commented on related market dynamics. Franklin Templeton observed that gold and silver are facing challenges but expressed a positive view on mining company equities. Heraeus noted that unclear employment statistics from the United States are making forecasts for gold more difficult, while also stating that elevated silver prices are attracting additional supply to the market.
